title: argentinean blackeye pea casserole
description: A savory, comforting casserole that brings together the flavors of argentinean cuisine. Made with blackeye peas, chorizo, and a creamy bechamel sauce, this dish is sure to become a family favorite.
instructions: In a large skillet, cook the chorizo over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the onion and garlic to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the canned diced tomatoes, chicken broth, flour, salt, black pepper, and paprika. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 2-3 minutes or until slightly thickened.
In a separate saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook for 1-2 minutes or until lightly golden.
Slowly pour in the milk, whisking constantly, and bring the mixture to a simmer. Cook for 2-3 minutes or until the sauce thickens.
Stir in the grated cheddar cheese until melted and smooth.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked blackeye peas, chorizo mixture, and bechamel sauce. Stir until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish and top with additional grated cheese if desired.
Bake for 25-30 minutes or until the casserole is golden brown and hot throughout.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with chopped parsley. Serve hot and enjoy!
Let the casserole rest for 10-15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
